name: Black Truffle Brioche French Toast with Foie Gras Custard, Compressed Berries & Maple-Sauternes Espuma
description: A decadent fine-dining brunch composition featuring brioche soaked in a foie gras-enriched custard, caramelized in beurre noisette, paired with vacuum-compressed berries, maple-Sauternes espuma, and finished with fresh black truffle and textural frozen fruit shards.
keywords: truffle french toast, foie gras custard, michelin brunch, compressed berries, maple espuma, molecular gastronomy, fine dining breakfast

steps.instruction: Prepare foie gras custard: Gently heat foie gras with cream until just melted. Blend until completely smooth. Whisk in egg yolks, sugar, salt, white pepper, and cognac. Pass through a fine chinois. Vacuum seal and cook in a precision immersion circulator at 63°C (145°F) for 45 minutes. Cool slightly.

Prepare compressed berries: Toss strawberries and raspberries with sugar, orange zest, and aged balsamic. Vacuum seal in a chamber vacuum sealer on high pressure to compress and intensify flavor. Chill.
Prepare maple-Sauternes espuma: Warm maple syrup and Sauternes together. Add cream and soy lecithin, then blend. Transfer to a Gourmet Whip, charge with 1 N2O cartridge, and hold warm.
Soak brioche: Submerge brioche slices in warm foie gras custard for 10–15 minutes until fully saturated but intact.
Cook French toast: Heat clarified butter in a heavy pan to 180°C (356°F). Sear brioche until deeply golden on both sides. Baste with brown butter to finish.
Prepare frozen berry shards: Blend a portion of compressed berries into a smooth purée. Spread thinly onto an anti-griddle to instantly freeze, then break into irregular shards.

Plating: Place French toast off-center. Arrange compressed berries around. Dispense maple-Sauternes espuma in controlled quenelles. Insert frozen berry shards vertically for height. Finish with freshly shaved black truffle, edible flowers, flaky sea salt, and optional gold leaf.

winePairings.name: Sauternes
winePairings.notes: Echoes the sweetness of the maple espuma while balancing foie gras richness with acidity.

winePairings.name: Vintage Champagne
winePairings.notes: Cuts through the fat and enhances the aromatic truffle and berry acidity.
